LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC. DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. THE MIRKOVICH DECLARATION IS NOT ADMISSIBLE FOR IT IS BASED ON INFORMATION AND BELIEF, NOT ON PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE. DEFENDANT SERVED A BILL OF PARTICULARS AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO RESPOND. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THE FRESE DECLARATION DOES NOT ESTABLISH AN OPEN BOOK ACCOUNT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert Resnor, Esq., specially appearing for Stephen M. Kappos, attorney for plaintiff / moving party Creditors Trade Association Inc. dba Great Western Collection Bureau
Matthew H. Weiner, Esq. of Law Offices of Mark C. Watson 650-692-4001, appearing for defendant / opposing party Gary Mirkovich aka Gary A. Mirkovich, ind. dba ICR Plumbing

Ruling:
PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC. dba Great Western Collection Bureau's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion is denied. Plaintiff failed to shift the burden of producing evidence. The Mirkovich Declaration is not admissible for it is based on information and belief, not on personal knowledge. Defendant served a bill of particulars and plaintiff failed to respond. As a consequence, the Frese Declaration does not establish an open book account. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600 =(302/PJM/PB)
